Now let me compile all of this into a comprehensive summary:

## Comprehensive Codebase Architecture Analysis

### 1. Top-Level Directory Structure

```
social-poster/
├── README.md                          # Detailed documentation
├── .git/                              # Version control
├── .vscode/                           # VS Code settings
├── .specstory/                        # Specstory development history
├── supabase/                          # Database migrations
└── worker/                            # Main Node.js application
```

### 2. Technology Stack

**Language & Runtime:**
- Node.js 18+ (ES2022, ESM modules via `"type": "module"`)
- TypeScript 5.9.3 (compiled to ES2022)

**Web Framework:**
- Hono 4.6.0 (lightweight HTTP framework with middleware support)
- @hono/node-server 1.13.0 (Node.js HTTP server adapter)

**Database:**
- Supabase (@supabase/supabase-js 2.99.3) for PostgreSQL access
- PostgREST for query operations

**Social Media:**
- twitter-api-v2 1.29.0 (X API v2 client for posting and OAuth)

**Image Generation & Processing:**
- OpenAI Images API (gpt-image-1, alternative: Google Imagen/Gemini)
- Satori 0.10.14 (JSX-to-SVG converter for rendering React components)
- @resvg/resvg-js 2.6.2 (SVG to PNG rendering)
- Sharp 0.34.5 (image processing, composition, format conversion)
- @jsquash/avif 2.1.1 (AVIF/HEIF image format support)
- React 18.3.1 (JSX component definition for graphics)

**Storage:**
- AWS SDK S3 Client (@aws-sdk/client-s3 3.750.0) for Cloudflare R2

**Development:**
- tsx 4.21.0 (TypeScript execution with watch mode)
- dotenv 16.6.1 (environment variable loading)

### 3. Build System & Scripts

**Build Commands (in `worker/package.json`):**
```
build:    tsc                    # TypeScript → dist/
start:    node dist/index.js     # Production run
dev:      tsx watch src/index.ts # Development with hot reload
```

**Deployment (Railway):**
- `railway.toml` configures build via NIXPACKS
- Build: `npm run build`
- Start: `npm start`
- Restart policy: ON_FAILURE with max 3 retries

**TypeScript Config (`tsconfig.json`):**
- Target: ES2022
- Module: NodeNext (ESM)
- Strict mode enabled
- Output: `dist/` directory

### 4. Architecture & Key Components

**Entry Point: `/worker/src/index.ts`**
- Loads `.env` via dotenv
- Initializes Hono HTTP server (PORT from env, default 3000)
- Starts background poller
- Logs admin UI URL

**Core Modules:**

1. **`server.ts`** (988 lines) - HTTP REST API
   - Hono middleware for Bearer token auth (ADMIN_SECRET)
   - Routes:
     - `GET /health` - health check
     - `GET /favicon.ico` - SVG favicon
     - `GET /admin` - static HTML admin UI
     - `GET /api/posts` - fetch queue/published posts with optional match data
     - `PATCH /api/posts/:id` - update post caption, status, scheduling, hashtags, bg_image_url
     - `POST /api/posts` - create scheduled post (manual)
     - `GET /api/jobs/plan` - preview planning job results
     - `POST /api/jobs/plan` - execute league-driven post planning
     - `POST /api/announcements/{kind}` - create announcement posts (registration, draft, results, playoffs, champion, awards, schedule)
     - `GET /api/announcements/:kind/preview-prompt` - preview AI prompts without generation
     - `GET /api/x/oauth/request` - initiate X 3-legged OAuth flow
     - `GET /api/x/oauth/callback` - X OAuth callback (signed HttpOnly cookie)
     - `GET /api/policies` - fetch leagues and policies
     - `PATCH /api/policies/:league_id/webhook_config` - update per-league X tokens
     - `GET /api/studio/suggestions` - fetch announcement content suggestions

2. **`poller.ts`** (172 lines) - Background polling service
   - Every 30 seconds: Fetch up to 10 pending/scheduled posts from Supabase
   - Optimistic locking: update status to `processing` only if status matches (prevents concurrent processing)
   - Flow per post:
     a. Generate game card PNG (for `verified_game` with `match_id`)
     b. Generate AI background image (for post types like `final_score`, `player_of_game`, `weekly_power_rankings`, `announcement_*`)
     c. Resolve post body (caption)
     d. Publish to X API
     e. Update status to `published` (or retry on error)
   - Every 60 seconds: Reset stuck `processing` rows (older than 2 minutes) back to `pending`

3. **`publisher.ts`** (179 lines) - X API interaction
   - X app credentials from `X_API_KEY` / `X_API_SECRET`
   - Per-league user tokens from Supabase `webhook_config` (fallback to env `X_ACCESS_TOKEN` / `X_ACCESS_SECRET`)
   - X client caching by league ID
   - Tweet posting with optional media upload
   - Media fetch and PNG upload to X v1 API
   - Detailed error handling with hints for OAuth, billing, token issues

4. **`card-generator.ts`** (31KB) - Game card & announcement graphic rendering
   - Uses Satori to render React components to SVG
   - Resvg to convert SVG to PNG
   - Supports:
     - Game score cards (verified_game)
     - AI background composition (overlay text/logos on AI-generated images)
     - Announcement graphics (registration, draft, results, playoffs, champion, awards, schedule)
   - 1200×630 px output (16:10 aspect ratio)
   - Inter font (Regular 400, Bold 700) loaded from `worker/fonts/`
   - Image processing via Sharp (resize, crop, convert formats)

5. **`templates.ts`** (9KB) - Post body copy generation
   - `resolvePostBody()` - route post type to copy builder
   - Game result captions: fetch match data, resolve team names/scores from Supabase
   - Final score, player of game, power rankings captions
   - Announcement captions (via `announcements/templates.ts`)
   - Custom payload body fallback
   - CTA/hashtags appended via `buildFinalCaption()`

6. **`r2.ts`** (123 lines) - Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible) storage
   - Bucket name parsing (first path segment) + optional prefix
   - S3Client endpoint auto-build from `R2_ACCOUNT_ID` or explicit `R2_ENDPOINT`
   - Credential swap fix (if access key is 64 chars and secret 32 chars, they're reversed)
   - PNG upload with public URL generation
   - Detailed error messages for Access Denied, NoSuchBucket

7. **`db.ts`** (7 lines) - Supabase client singleton
   - Initialized with `SUPABASE_URL` and `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY`

8. **`xOAuth.ts`** (72 lines) - X 3-legged OAuth 1.0a
   - Sign/verify JWT-like tokens in HttpOnly cookies
   - 15-minute expiration
   - Binding league_id to pending OAuth request
   - Secure cookie flag (production only)

9. **`leagueLogo.ts`** (2KB) - League asset resolution
   - Fetch league logo URLs from Supabase `leagues_info`
   - Asset URL conversion (handle CORS, CDN patterns)

### AI Image Generation (`/ai/` directory)

1. **`imageClient.ts`** - Multi-provider dispatch
   - OpenAI Images API (gpt-image-1, 1024×1536 default)
   - Google Imagen/Gemini (via `imagenClient.ts`)
   - Retry logic with 2 retries
   - 60-second timeout
   - Supports b64_json or URL response formats

2. **`imagenClient.ts`** - Google Imagen/Gemini integration
   - Custom base URL and model/aspect-ratio overrides

3. **`generateBackground.ts`** (102 lines)
   - Build prompt via `buildBgPrompt()`
   - Generate image via provider
   - Composite onto AI background (overlay text/logos via Satori)
   - Upload to R2 under `social-poster/bg/{stylePack}/{cacheKey}.png`
   - Cache key includes post type, style pack, match/season id, vibe

4. **`bgPrompts.ts`** (80+ lines, 10KB content)
   - 6 style packs: `regular`, `playoffs`, `rivalry`, `sponsor_safe`, `regular_season_launch`, `stage_combine_playground`
   - Detailed cinematic prompt templates for sports broadcast aesthetic
   - LBA brand color directive (navy, purple, gold)
   - Overridable via `AI_IMAGE_BRAND_RULES` env
   - Announcement-specific scenes from `announcements/templates.ts`

5. **`hash.ts`** - SHA256 for cache keys

### Planning & Automation (`/planning/` directory)

1. **`planPosts.ts`** (159 lines)
   - `planPosts()` - main entry point (called by `POST /api/jobs/plan`)
   - Inserts scheduled X posts for:
     - **final_score**: Recent completed matches (+30 min delay)
     - **player_of_game**: Matches with MVP (+35 min delay)
     - **weekly_power_rankings**: Top 10 teams (next scheduled power rankings time)
   - Requires `LEAGUE_ID` env
   - Deduplication via payload matching

2. **`queries.ts`** (12KB)
   - Supabase queries:
     - `fetchCompletedMatches()` - recent verified matches
     - `fetchMatchesWithMvp()` - matches with player MVP
     - `fetchTop10PowerRankings()` - league standings filtered by `lba_teams`
     - `existsScheduledByPayload()` - deduplication check
     - `insertPlannedXPost()` - insert scheduled_posts row
     - `existsAnnouncementScheduled()` - check for duplicate announcements
     - `fetchStudioSuggestions()` - fetch dynamic content for UI

3. **`payloads.ts`** (types for final_score, player_of_game, power_rankings)

### Announcements (`/announcements/` directory)

1. **`templates.ts`** (12KB)
   - 7 announcement kinds: registration, draft, results, playoffs, champion, awards, schedule
   - 4 vibes: esports_2k, luxury, hype, broadcast (+ championship, cartoon_modern)
   - Captions per kind/vibe
   - Midjourney export prompt generation
   - AI scene fragments (abstract backgrounds)
   - Background composition rules

### Types & Payloads

**`types.ts`:**
- `ScheduledPost` - database row type
- `ScheduledPostPayload` - JSON payload (generic key-value, must be non-null in DB)
  - Game data: `match_id`, team names, scores, dates
  - Player data: player name, stats, team
  - Rankings data: week_label, teams array
  - Announcement data: season, draft_date, combine_dates, prize_pool, cta, vibe, etc.
  - AI metadata: `ai_bg_prompt`, `ai_bg_generated_at`
  - Style overrides: `style_pack`, `style_version`, `generate_image` boolean

### Database Schema (Supabase)

**Key Tables:**
- `scheduled_posts` - post queue (id, post_type, status, match_id, caption, publish_surface, payload_json, retries, x_post_id, error, etc.)
- `webhook_config` - per-league settings (x_access_token, x_access_secret)
- `matches` - game data (team_a_id, team_b_id, score_a, score_b, league_id, etc.)
- `teams` - team info (name, logo_url, etc.)
- `players` - player data
- `player_stats` - match statistics
- `match_mvp` - MVP assignments
- `leagues_info` - league metadata (logo_url, name, etc.)
- `post_policies` - automation flags
- `lba_teams` - filter for power rankings
- `league_conference_standings` - standings data
- `league_seasons` - season automation (trigger on `is_active = true`)

**Automation Trigger:**
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/20260320140000_league_season_announcement_posts.sql`
- On season activation: automatically inserts 3 announcement post rows

### Configuration & Environment

**Required Variables:**
- `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` - database
- `X_API_KEY`, `X_API_SECRET` - X app credentials
- `X_ACCESS_TOKEN`, `X_ACCESS_SECRET` - default user tokens (fallback)
- `ADMIN_SECRET` - Bearer token + OAuth signing key
- `PORT` - HTTP listen port (default 3000)

**Optional Variables:**
- `X_OAUTH_CALLBACK_URL` - X OAuth callback (auto-derived from request origin if not set)
- `R2_ENDPOINT` / `R2_ACCOUNT_ID` - Cloudflare R2 S3 endpoint
- `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` / `R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` - R2 credentials
- `R2_BUCKET` - bucket name or bucket/prefix
- `R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL` - public URL base for R2 objects
- `LEAGUE_ID` - league UUID for planning job
- `PLAN_DEFAULT_STYLE_PACK` - style pack for planned posts (default: regular)
- `POWER_RANKINGS_DAY` / `POWER_RANKINGS_HOUR` - schedule (0=Sun, 18=6pm)
- `AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER` - openai or gemini (default: openai)
- `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_BASE_URL`, `OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL`, `OPENAI_IMAGE_SIZE`, `OPENAI_IMAGE_QUALITY`
- `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_BASE_URL`, `GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL`, `GEMINI_IMAGE_ASPECT_RATIO`
- `AI_IMAGE_BRAND_RULES` - custom brand directive for prompts

### Admin UI (`public/admin.html`)

**Static SPA** with tabs:
- **Queue** - pending, processing, scheduled, draft posts
- **Published** - published, failed posts
- **Studio** - create/preview announcements (registration, draft, results, playoffs, champion, awards, schedule)
- **Policies** - link X accounts per league (OAuth), manage league settings

### Key Patterns & Conventions

1. **Optimistic Locking** - POST status updates only succeed if status matches initial fetch
2. **Deduplication** - payload-based (match_id, week_label, season_id + vibe)
3. **Retry Logic** - up to 3 attempts; auto-reset stuck `processing` rows after 2 minutes
4. **Lazy Client Caching** - X clients cached by league ID, invalidated after OAuth
5. **JSX-based Graphics** - React components → Satori → SVG → Resvg → PNG
6. **Style Packs** - pre-written prompt templates per aesthetic theme
7. **Announcement Automation** - Supabase trigger inserts posts when season is activated
8. **Bearer Token Auth** - all `/api/*` routes except `GET /api/x/oauth/callback` require `Authorization: Bearer ADMIN_SECRET`

### Recent Activity

**Latest Commit (Mar 21):**
"Enhance announcement features and UI for playoffs, champion, awards, and schedule"

**Commit History:**
- Announcement UI enhancements (registration, draft, results, playoffs, champion, awards, schedule)
- Session statistics & README updates
- League announcement features & AI image generation
- Admin UI & AI image generation functionality
- Statistics timestamp consistency

 `./README.md`

```text
     1→# social-poster
     2→
     3→A single **Node.js** process on Railway (or elsewhere) that:
     4→
     5→1. **Polls** Supabase `scheduled_posts` on a timer, claims rows with an optimistic lock, builds captions (including game-result copy from `matches` / `player_stats`), optionally generates a **game card** image (Satori + Resvg) for `verified_game`, optionally generates an **AI background** (OpenAI Images or Google Imagen) for `final_score` / `player_of_game` / `weekly_power_rankings`, uploads images to **Cloudflare R2**, attaches media when URLs are present, posts via the **X API**, and updates row status with retries.
     6→2. **Serves** a small **Hono** HTTP server on `PORT`: `GET /admin` (static HTML UI), `GET /health`, and JSON APIs under `/api/*` protected by a shared **`ADMIN_SECRET`** (Bearer token) — except **`GET /api/x/oauth/callback`**, which X redirects to after OAuth — including **`POST /api/jobs/plan`** to enqueue league-driven posts (cron-friendly).
     7→
     8→The runnable package lives under [`worker/`](./worker/) (`package.json` name: `social-publisher`).
     9→
    10→## Requirements
    11→
    12→- **Node.js** 18+ (ES2022; `fetch` and modern APIs; ESM via `"type": "module"`)
    13→- **Supabase** with the expected tables (see below)
    14→- **X Developer** app credentials (API key/secret; user tokens per environment or per league)
    15→- **Cloudflare R2** (optional) — needed for auto-generated PNG cards (`verified_game`) and for **AI background** images (stored under `social-poster/bg/…` on your bucket)
    16→- **OpenAI** and/or **Google (Gemini / Imagen)** API keys — only if you use AI image generation (`AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER`)
    17→
    18→## Quick start
    19→
    20→```bash
    21→cd worker
    22→pnpm install   # or: npm install
    23→cp .env.example .env   # create .env — see Environment variables
    24→pnpm run build
    25→pnpm start
    26→```
    27→
    28→The entrypoint loads `worker/.env` automatically via `dotenv` (so `pnpm dev` / `pnpm start` pick up `SUPABASE_URL` and other vars without exporting them in the shell).
    29→
    30→Then open `http://localhost:3000/admin` (or your Railway URL + `/admin`) and sign in with `ADMIN_SECRET`.
    31→
    32→For local development with reload, use `pnpm run dev` (runs `tsx watch src/index.ts`).
    33→
    34→## Environment variables
    35→
    36→| Variable | Purpose |
    37→| -------- | ------- |
    38→| `SUPABASE_URL` | Supabase project URL |
    39→| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` | Service role key (bypasses RLS for the worker **and** the admin API) |
    40→| `X_API_KEY` | X app API key |
    41→| `X_API_SECRET` | X app API secret |
    42→| `X_ACCESS_TOKEN` | Default user access token (used when no per-league tokens exist) |
    43→| `X_ACCESS_SECRET` | Default user access token secret |
    44→| `PORT` | HTTP listen port (Railway sets this automatically) |
    45→| `ADMIN_SECRET` | Bearer token required for most `/api/*` routes, used to sign OAuth state cookies, and used by the admin UI login |
    46→| `X_OAUTH_CALLBACK_URL` | Optional. Full URL of `GET /api/x/oauth/callback` (must match the callback registered on your [X app](https://developer.x.com/)). If omitted, the worker uses `{request Origin}/api/x/oauth/callback` (fine for local dev if you register that URL). |
    47→| `R2_ENDPOINT` | Optional. Full S3 API URL, e.g. `https://<account-id>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com`. If set, used instead of building from `R2_ACCOUNT_ID` (same pattern as the lba-social worker). |
    48→| `R2_ACCOUNT_ID` | Used only when `R2_ENDPOINT` is unset, to build `https://<id>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com`. |
    49→| `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` / `R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | R2 S3 API token with **Object Read & Write**. If lengths look reversed (64 / 32 chars), the worker swaps them like lba-social. |
    50→| `R2_BUCKET` | **lba-social semantics:** first `/`-separated segment is the **bucket name**; any further segments are an **object key prefix** prepended to uploads (e.g. `my-bucket` or `my-bucket/graphics/lba`). Not “last segment = bucket”. |
    51→| `R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL` | Public base URL for objects (no trailing slash), e.g. `https://pub-xxx.r2.dev` |
    52→| `LEAGUE_ID` | League UUID (`leagues_info.id`) — required for **`POST /api/jobs/plan`**; optional default for `payload_json.league_id` on **`POST /api/posts`** |
    53→| `PLAN_DEFAULT_STYLE_PACK` | Style preset for planned posts (`regular`, `playoffs`, …) — default `regular` |
    54→| `POWER_RANKINGS_DAY` / `POWER_RANKINGS_HOUR` | When the plan job targets the next power-rankings post (day: 0=Sun; hour local server TZ) |
    55→| `AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER` | `openai` (default) or `gemini` (Imagen) |
    56→| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | Required when `AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER=openai` |
    57→| `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | Optional override (default `https://api.openai.com/v1`) |
    58→| `OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL` / `OPENAI_IMAGE_SIZE` / `OPENAI_IMAGE_QUALITY` | Optional OpenAI Images API tuning |
    59→| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | Required when `AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER=gemini` |
    60→| `GEMINI_BASE_URL` / `GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL` / `GEMINI_IMAGE_ASPECT_RATIO` | Optional Imagen tuning |
    61→| `AI_IMAGE_BRAND_RULES` | Optional plain-text prompt suffix replacing the default LBA color directive |
    62→
    63→**Per-league X tokens (optional):** If `webhook_config` has `x_access_token` and `x_access_secret` for a `league_id`, those are used instead of the default env tokens for posts tied to that league (resolved via `payload_json.league_id` or `matches.league_id`).
    64→
    65→### Linking X accounts (3-legged OAuth 1.0a)
    66→
    67→The admin **Policies** tab has **Link X** per league. That flow follows [X’s 3-legged OAuth](https://docs.x.com/fundamentals/authentication/oauth-1-0a/obtaining-user-access-tokens): request token → user authorizes on X → callback exchanges the verifier for access token + secret, which are written to `webhook_config` for that league.
    68→
    69→1. In the [X developer portal](https://developer.x.com/), add **Callback / Redirect URL** exactly matching your deployed callback (e.g. `https://<your-host>/api/x/oauth/callback`), or the value of `X_OAUTH_CALLBACK_URL` if you set it.
    70→2. Use **Read and Write** (or higher) app permissions so posting works.
    71→3. Set a non-default **`ADMIN_SECRET`** — it signs the short-lived pending-OAuth cookie (the callback route is not Bearer-protected; the signed cookie binds the league to the request token).
    72→
    73→**Security:** Most admin APIs use `ADMIN_SECRET` as Bearer token. `GET /api/x/oauth/callback` is called by X’s redirect and uses an HttpOnly cookie instead. The Supabase key is highly privileged — treat `.env` and Railway secrets accordingly.
    74→
    75→Create a `.env` file in `worker/` with these values. Do not commit secrets.
    76→
    77→## How it behaves
    78→
    79→1. **Poll** — Every **30 seconds**, loads up to 10 rows from `scheduled_posts` where `status` is `pending` or `scheduled`, due (`scheduled_for` null or past), `retries` &lt; 3, and `publish_surface` contains `x`.
    80→2. **Lock** — Updates the row to `processing` only if `status` is still the value seen when fetched; if the update returns no row, another replica claimed it — skip.
    81→3. **Game card (optional)** — For `verified_game` with `match_id` and no `bg_image_url`, if R2 is configured, generates a PNG, uploads it, and sets `bg_image_url` before publish. On failure, continues text-only.
    82→4. **AI background (optional)** — For `final_score`, `player_of_game`, `weekly_power_rankings`, and **`announcement_registration` / `announcement_draft` / `announcement_results`** with no `bg_image_url` and `payload_json.generate_image !== false`, if R2 and the chosen image API are configured, generates a plate, **composites team/league logos and headline text** (Satori + Sharp) at **1200×630** (not 1:1 — use external tools like Midjourney if you need square assets), uploads to R2, and sets `bg_image_url` (metadata in `payload_json.ai_bg_*`). Final-score graphics resolve names and logos from `matches` / `teams` when `payload_json.match_id` is set. Announcement plates are **text-free** in the model; copy is drawn via Satori (same pattern as scores). On failure, continues text-only.
    83→5. **Caption** — From `caption`, or built from match data for `verified_game`, or template text for planned post types and `announcement_*` types (`worker/src/announcements/templates.ts`), or `payload_json.body`, plus CTA/hashtags.
    84→6. **Publish** — X media from `boxscore_processed_feed_url`, `bg_image_url`, or `asset_urls[0]` (PNG). Tweet id stored in `x_post_id`.
    85→7. **Retries** — On error, `retries` increments; after 3 attempts, `status` becomes `failed` and `error` is set.
    86→8. **Stuck rows** — Every **60 seconds**, `processing` rows older than **2 minutes** reset to `pending` (X-targeting rows only).
    87→
    88→**Operations:** OpenAI / Imagen calls can take **30–60s** or more. They run inside the poller loop (batch size 10, interval 30s), so avoid running many concurrent AI posts on a single small instance if you hit timeouts.
    89→
    90→### Planning job (`POST /api/jobs/plan`)
    91→
    92→Runs once when called (use Railway Cron or another scheduler). Requires **`LEAGUE_ID`**. Inserts **`scheduled`** rows with `publish_surface = ['x']` and **`match_id` left null** (so they do not conflict with the unique X index on `match_id` used by `verified_game` auto-posts). Deduplication uses `payload_json.match_id` or `week_label` like the source worker.
    93→
    94→- **final_score** — recent verified matches (+30 min)
    95→- **player_of_game** — matches with `match_mvp` (+35 min)
    96→- **weekly_power_rankings** — top 10 from `league_conference_standings` filtered by **`lba_teams`** (same as lba-social); failures in this block are reported in the JSON response but do not fail the whole job
    97→
    98→### `POST /api/posts` (extended)
    99→
   100→Optional JSON fields: `match_id`, `payload_json`, `generate_image`, `style_pack`, `style_version`, `league_id`, `bg_image_url`, `asset_urls`. If `LEAGUE_ID` is set in the environment and the body omits `payload_json.league_id`, the env value is copied into `payload_json` for X token resolution.
   101→
   102→### League announcements (`POST /api/announcements/*`)
   103→
   104→Authenticated like other admin APIs (`Authorization: Bearer ADMIN_SECRET`).
   105→
   106→| Route | `post_type` |
   107→| ----- | ----------- |
   108→| `POST /api/announcements/registration` | `announcement_registration` |
   109→| `POST /api/announcements/draft` | `announcement_draft` |
   110→| `POST /api/announcements/results` | `announcement_results` |
   111→
   112→**Body (JSON)** — common fields:
   113→
   114→- **`season`** (string, required) — e.g. `Season 2`
   115→- **`cta`** (string, required) — URL or host path, e.g. `lba.gg/signup/player`
   116→- **`league_id`** (UUID, required unless `LEAGUE_ID` is set in the worker env)
   117→- **`season_id`** (UUID, optional) — `league_seasons.id`; used with `league_id` and `post_type` for **deduplication** (returns **409** if a non-failed row already exists, unless `skip_dedupe: true` or `force: true`)
   118→- **`draft_date`**, **`combine_dates`**, **`prize_pool`** (optional strings)
   119→- **`cta_label`**, **`league_logo`**, **`headline_override`**, **`vibe`** (`esports_2k` \| `luxury` \| `hype`)
   120→- **`result_lines`** (string array, optional; mainly for `results`)
   121→- Same scheduling/caption flags as `POST /api/posts`: `scheduled_for`, `draft`, `caption`, `hashtags`, `generate_image`, `style_pack`, `style_version`
   122→
   123→Example:
   124→
   125→```json
   126→{
   127→  "season": "Season 2",
   128→  "season_id": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
   129→  "draft_date": "April 3",
   130→  "combine_dates": "March 27–29",
   131→  "prize_pool": "$1500",
   132→  "cta": "lba.gg/signup/player",
   133→  "vibe": "esports_2k"
   134→}
   135→```
   136→
   137→**Preview (no image API call):** `GET /api/announcements/:kind/preview-prompt?season=Season%202&cta=…&vibe=esports_2k` — returns `ai_image_prompt`, `midjourney_prompt_export`, and `default_caption`.
   138→
   139→**Admin:** Studio tab **Announcements**, or the “New announcement” form (text-only types remain; AI announcements are easiest from Studio).
   140→
   141→### Season automation (Supabase)
   142→
   143→Migration [`supabase/migrations/20260320140000_league_season_announcement_posts.sql`](supabase/migrations/20260320140000_league_season_announcement_posts.sql) defines a trigger on **`public.league_seasons`**: when **`is_active`** becomes **true** (and was not already active), it inserts three **`scheduled_posts`** rows (`announcement_registration`, `announcement_draft`, `announcement_results`) with `publish_surface = ['x']`, `match_id` null, `payload_json` built from `league_seasons` + `leagues_info` (`lg_logo_url`, `lg_url` for CTA, `prize_pool`, `season_number`, dates). **Apply that migration in your own Supabase project** (this repo does not run migrations remotely). Adjust the function if your column names differ.
   144→
   145→## Database touchpoints
   146→
   147→- `scheduled_posts` — scheduling, surfaces, captions, media URLs, `match_id`, `payload_json` (NOT NULL on insert), `retries`, etc.
   148→- `webhook_config` — per-league `x_access_token` / `x_access_secret`
   149→- `matches`, `teams`, `player_stats`, `match_mvp` — game captions and card art
   150→- `leagues_info`, `post_policies` — admin policies UI and automation flags
   151→- `players`, `lba_teams`, `league_conference_standings` — used only by the **plan** job (power rankings needs `lba_teams`; omit or fix env if those objects are absent in your project)
   152→- `league_seasons` — optional trigger (see migration above) to enqueue announcement posts when a season is activated
   153→
   154→AI prompt metadata is stored in **`payload_json`** (`ai_bg_prompt`, `ai_bg_generated_at`, `style_pack`, …) — no extra `scheduled_posts` columns are required.
   155→
   156→Schema changes belong in your Supabase migration workflow; this repo does not run migrations against your project.
   157→
   158→## Scripts (`worker/package.json`)
   159→
   160→| Script | Command |
   161→| ------ | ------- |
   162→| `build` | `tsc` → `dist/` |
   163→| `start` | `node dist/index.js` |
   164→| `dev` | `tsx watch src/index.ts` |
   165→
   166→## Project layout
   167→
   168→```
   169→social-poster/
   170→├── README.md
   171→└── worker/
   172→    ├── package.json
   173→    ├── tsconfig.json
   174→    ├── railway.toml
   175→    ├── public/
   176→    │   └── admin.html
   177→    ├── fonts/
   178→    │   ├── Inter-Regular.ttf
   179→    │   └── Inter-Bold.ttf
   180→    └── src/
   181→        ├── index.ts          # HTTP server + start poller
   182→        ├── poller.ts
   183→        ├── server.ts
   184→        ├── db.ts
   185→        ├── types.ts
   186→        ├── templates.ts
   187→        ├── publisher.ts
   188→        ├── card-generator.ts
   189→        ├── r2.ts
   190→        ├── announcements/    # league announcement copy + AI scene fragments + MJ export helper
   191→        ├── ai/               # OpenAI / Imagen + prompts + R2 upload
   192→        └── planning/         # plan job + Supabase queries
   193→```
   194→
   195→## Troubleshooting (X API)
   196→
   197→- **`Request failed with code 401` / `Unauthorized`:** OAuth 1.0a user context was rejected. Common causes: **Access Token + Secret were issued for a different X app** than `X_API_KEY` / `X_API_SECRET`; tokens **expired** or were **regenerated**; app permissions are **read-only** (need **Read and Write** to post); or you’re using **per-league** `webhook_config` tokens that are wrong for the app keys in `.env`. Regenerate **User authentication** tokens in the [developer portal](https://developer.x.com/) under the same app that owns your Consumer Key/Secret.
   198→- **`Request failed with code 402` / `[x tweet] … 402`:** Returned by X’s HTTP API when the request is rejected for **billing or access tier** reasons (wording varies). Confirm in the [X developer portal](https://developer.x.com/) that your project has the right **product / plan**, that **posting** is allowed for your app, and that user tokens are still valid. It is not caused by this worker’s scheduling logic.
   199→- **`[x media] …`:** Failure while **downloading** the image URL or **uploading** media to X; check that the URL is public (e.g. R2) and that the file is valid PNG for the current upload path.
   200→
   201→Errors are stored on `scheduled_posts.error` and logged by the poller with a `[x tweet]` or `[x media]` prefix when applicable.
   202→

   204→

   206→

 `./worker/src/poller.ts`

```text
     1→import {
     2→  generateBackgroundForPost,
     3→  isAiImagePostType,
     4→} from './ai/generateBackground.js'
     5→import { generateGameCard } from './card-generator.js'
     6→import { supabase } from './db.js'
     7→import { publishToX } from './publisher.js'
     8→import { isR2Configured } from './r2.js'
     9→import { resolvePostBody } from './templates.js'
    10→import type { ScheduledPost } from './types.js'
    11→
    12→function shouldGenerateAiBg(post: ScheduledPost): boolean {
    13→  if (!isAiImagePostType(post.post_type)) return false
    14→  if (post.bg_image_url) return false
    15→  if (post.payload_json.generate_image === false) return false
    16→  return true
    17→}
    18→
    19→async function maybeGenerateAiBackground(
    20→  working: ScheduledPost
    21→): Promise<ScheduledPost> {
    22→  if (!shouldGenerateAiBg(working)) return working
    23→  if (!isR2Configured()) {
    24→    console.warn('[poller] AI image skipped: R2 not configured')
    25→    return working
    26→  }
    27→  const p = working.payload_json as Record<string, unknown>
    28→  const stylePack =
    29→    typeof p.style_pack === 'string' && p.style_pack.trim()
    30→      ? p.style_pack.trim()
    31→      : 'regular'
    32→  const styleVersion =
    33→    typeof p.style_version === 'number' && Number.isFinite(p.style_version)
    34→      ? p.style_version
    35→      : 1
    36→  try {
    37→    const { imageUrl, prompt } = await generateBackgroundForPost({
    38→      postType: working.post_type,
    39→      stylePack,
    40→      styleVersion,
    41→      payload: p,
    42→    })
    43→    const nextPayload = {
    44→      ...working.payload_json,
    45→      ai_bg_prompt: prompt,
    46→      ai_bg_generated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    47→    }
    48→    await supabase
    49→      .from('scheduled_posts')
    50→      .update({ bg_image_url: imageUrl, payload_json: nextPayload })
    51→      .eq('id', working.id)
    52→    return {
    53→      ...working,
    54→      bg_image_url: imageUrl,
    55→      payload_json: nextPayload,
    56→    }
    57→  } catch (e) {
    58→    console.warn('[poller] AI background failed, continuing text-only:', e)
    59→    return working
    60→  }
    61→}
    62→
    63→async function processPendingPosts() {
    64→  const now = new Date().toISOString()
    65→
    66→  const { data: posts, error } = await supabase
    67→    .from('scheduled_posts')
    68→    .select('*')
    69→    .in('status', ['pending', 'scheduled'])
    70→    .or(`scheduled_for.is.null,scheduled_for.lte.${now}`)
    71→    .lt('retries', 3)
    72→    .contains('publish_surface', ['x'])
    73→    .limit(10)
    74→
    75→  if (error) {
    76→    console.error('[poller] fetch error:', error.message)
    77→    return
    78→  }
    79→
    80→  for (const row of posts ?? []) {
    81→    const raw = row as Record<string, unknown>
    82→    const post: ScheduledPost = {
    83→      ...(raw as ScheduledPost),
    84→      payload_json:
    85→        raw.payload_json && typeof raw.payload_json === 'object'
    86→          ? (raw.payload_json as ScheduledPost['payload_json'])
    87→          : {},
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    const { data: claimed, error: lockErr } = await supabase
    91→      .from('scheduled_posts')
    92→      .update({ status: 'processing', updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
    93→      .eq('id', post.id)
    94→      .eq('status', post.status)
    95→      .select('id')
    96→
    97→    if (lockErr) {
    98→      console.error('[poller] lock error:', lockErr.message)
    99→      continue
   100→    }
   101→    if (!claimed?.length) continue
   102→
   103→    try {
   104→      let working: ScheduledPost = { ...post, status: 'processing' }
   105→
   106→      if (
   107→        working.post_type === 'verified_game' &&
   108→        !working.bg_image_url &&
   109→        working.match_id
   110→      ) {
   111→        try {
   112→          const cardUrl = await generateGameCard(working.match_id)
   113→          await supabase
   114→            .from('scheduled_posts')
   115→            .update({ bg_image_url: cardUrl })
   116→            .eq('id', working.id)
   117→          working = { ...working, bg_image_url: cardUrl }
   118→        } catch (e) {
   119→          console.warn('[poller] card gen failed, continuing text-only:', e)
   120→        }
   121→      }
   122→
   123→      working = await maybeGenerateAiBackground(working)
   124→
   125→      const body = await resolvePostBody(working)
   126→      const updates: Record<string, unknown> = {
   127→        status: 'published',
   128→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   129→      }
   130→
   131→      if ((working.publish_surface ?? []).includes('x')) {
   132→        updates.x_post_id = await publishToX(working, body)
   133→      }
   134→
   135→      await supabase.from('scheduled_posts').update(updates).eq('id', working.id)
   136→      console.log(`[poller] published post ${working.id}`)
   137→    } catch (err: unknown) {
   138→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)
   139→      const retries = (post.retries ?? 0) + 1
   140→      console.error(`[poller] failed post ${post.id} (attempt ${retries}):`, message)
   141→
   142→      await supabase
   143→        .from('scheduled_posts')
   144→        .update({
   145→          status: retries >= 3 ? 'failed' : 'pending',
   146→          retries,
   147→          error: message,
   148→          updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   149→        })
   150→        .eq('id', post.id)
   151→    }
   152→  }
   153→}
   154→
   155→async function resetStuckPosts() {
   156→  const cutoff = new Date(Date.now() - 2 * 60 * 1000).toISOString()
   157→
   158→  await supabase
   159→    .from('scheduled_posts')
   160→    .update({ status: 'pending', updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   161→    .eq('status', 'processing')
   162→    .lt('updated_at', cutoff)
   163→    .contains('publish_surface', ['x'])
   164→}
   165→
   166→export function startPoller() {
   167→  console.log('[poller] started')
   168→  setInterval(processPendingPosts, 30_000)
   169→  setInterval(resetStuckPosts, 60_000)
   170→  void processPendingPosts()
   171→}
   172→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import {
     2→  ApiRequestError,
     3→  ApiResponseError,
     4→  TwitterApi,
     5→} from 'twitter-api-v2'
     6→import { supabase } from './db.js'
     7→import type { ScheduledPost } from './types.js'
     8→
     9→export function getXAppConsumerKeys(): { appKey: string; appSecret: string } {
    10→  const appKey = process.env.X_API_KEY?.trim()
    11→  const appSecret = process.env.X_API_SECRET?.trim()
    12→  if (!appKey || !appSecret) {
    13→    throw new Error(
    14→      '[x] Set X_API_KEY and X_API_SECRET (Consumer Key / Secret from your X app in the developer portal).'
    15→    )
    16→  }
    17→  return { appKey, appSecret }
    18→}
    19→
    20→function formatXApiError(err: unknown): string {
    21→  if (err instanceof ApiResponseError) {
    22→    const payload =
    23→      err.data !== undefined && err.data !== null
    24→        ? (() => {
    25→            try {
    26→              return JSON.stringify(err.data)
    27→            } catch {
    28→              return String(err.data)
    29→            }
    30→          })()
    31→        : ''
    32→    const hint =
    33→      err.code === 401
    34→        ? ' (HTTP 401: OAuth rejected — regenerate User Access Token + Secret in the X developer portal for THIS app (must include Read + Write); tokens from another app or old keys will fail. If the post uses a league, set webhook_config x_access_token + x_access_secret for that league, or fix X_ACCESS_TOKEN / X_ACCESS_SECRET in .env.)'
    35→        : err.code === 402
    36→          ? ' (HTTP 402: X often uses this for billing/API access limits — check developer portal plan and app permissions.)'
    37→          : err.code === 403
    38→            ? ' (HTTP 403: forbidden — check app permissions / token scope / Basic vs paid tier.)'
    39→            : ''
    40→    return `${err.message}${payload ? ` | body: ${payload}` : ''}${hint}`
    41→  }
    42→  if (err instanceof ApiRequestError) {
    43→    return `${err.message} | underlying: ${err.requestError?.message ?? String(err.requestError)}`
    44→  }
    45→  if (err instanceof Error) return err.message
    46→  return String(err)
    47→}
    48→
    49→const xClientCache = new Map<string, TwitterApi>()
    50→
    51→/** Call after updating webhook_config X tokens for a league so the next post picks up new credentials. */
    52→export function invalidateXClientCacheForLeague(leagueId: string): void {
    53→  xClientCache.delete(leagueId)
    54→}
    55→
    56→/** Log once at boot if default user credentials are missing (per-league-only setups still need app keys). */
    57→export function warnXAuthOnBoot(): void {
    58→  const appKey = process.env.X_API_KEY?.trim()
    59→  const appSecret = process.env.X_API_SECRET?.trim()
    60→  if (!appKey || !appSecret) {
    61→    console.warn(
    62→      '[x] X_API_KEY / X_API_SECRET missing — posting to X will fail until app Consumer Key & Secret are set.'
    63→    )
    64→    return
    65→  }
    66→  const tok = process.env.X_ACCESS_TOKEN?.trim()
    67→  const sec = process.env.X_ACCESS_SECRET?.trim()
    68→  if (!tok || !sec) {
    69→    console.warn(
    70→      '[x] X_ACCESS_TOKEN / X_ACCESS_SECRET missing — only posts with per-league webhook_config (x_access_token + x_access_secret) can publish; announcements / unmatched leagues need .env user tokens.'
    71→    )
    72→  }
    73→}
    74→
    75→export async function getXClient(leagueId: string): Promise<TwitterApi | null> {
    76→  if (xClientCache.has(leagueId)) return xClientCache.get(leagueId)!
    77→
    78→  const { data: rows } = await supabase
    79→    .from('webhook_config')
    80→    .select('key, value')
    81→    .eq('league_id', leagueId)
    82→    .in('key', ['x_access_token', 'x_access_secret'])
    83→
    84→  if (!rows || rows.length < 2) return null
    85→
    86→  const get = (k: string) => rows.find(r => r.key === k)?.value
    87→  const token = get('x_access_token')
    88→  const secret = get('x_access_secret')
    89→  if (!token?.trim() || !secret?.trim()) return null
    90→
    91→  const { appKey, appSecret } = getXAppConsumerKeys()
    92→  const client = new TwitterApi({
    93→    appKey,
    94→    appSecret,
    95→    accessToken: token.trim(),
    96→    accessSecret: secret.trim(),
    97→  })
    98→
    99→  xClientCache.set(leagueId, client)
   100→  return client
   101→}
   102→
   103→export async function publishToX(post: ScheduledPost, body: string): Promise<string> {
   104→  let leagueId: string | undefined =
   105→    typeof post.payload_json.league_id === 'string'
   106→      ? post.payload_json.league_id
   107→      : undefined
   108→
   109→  if (!leagueId && post.match_id) {
   110→    const { data } = await supabase
   111→      .from('matches')
   112→      .select('league_id')
   113→      .eq('id', post.match_id)
   114→      .single()
   115→    leagueId = data?.league_id ?? undefined
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  const client = leagueId ? await getXClient(leagueId) : null
   119→
   120→  const { appKey, appSecret } = getXAppConsumerKeys()
   121→  let twitter: TwitterApi
   122→  if (client) {
   123→    twitter = client
   124→  } else {
   125→    const accessToken = process.env.X_ACCESS_TOKEN?.trim()
   126→    const accessSecret = process.env.X_ACCESS_SECRET?.trim()
   127→    if (!accessToken || !accessSecret) {
   128→      throw new Error(
   129→        `[x tweet] No per-league X tokens in webhook_config for league_id=${leagueId ?? '(none)'} ` +
   130→          'and X_ACCESS_TOKEN / X_ACCESS_SECRET are missing in the environment. ' +
   131→          'Add OAuth 1.0a user token+secret to .env, or add x_access_token + x_access_secret for this league.'
   132→      )
   133→    }
   134→    twitter = new TwitterApi({
   135→      appKey,
   136→      appSecret,
   137→      accessToken,
   138→      accessSecret,
   139→    })
   140→  }
   141→
   142→  let mediaId: string | undefined
   143→
   144→  const mediaUrl =
   145→    post.boxscore_processed_feed_url ??
   146→    post.bg_image_url ??
   147→    post.asset_urls?.[0]
   148→
   149→  if (mediaUrl) {
   150→    try {
   151→      const res = await fetch(mediaUrl)
   152→      if (!res.ok) {
   153→        throw new Error(
   154→          `[x media] fetch image ${mediaUrl.slice(0, 80)}… failed: HTTP ${res.status}`
   155→        )
   156→      }
   157→      const arrayBuf = await res.arrayBuffer()
   158→      const buf = Buffer.from(new Uint8Array(arrayBuf))
   159→      mediaId = await twitter.v1.uploadMedia(buf, { mimeType: 'image/png' })
   160→    } catch (err: unknown) {
   161→      throw new Error(
   162→        err instanceof Error && err.message.startsWith('[x media]')
   163→          ? err.message
   164→          : `[x media] ${formatXApiError(err)}`
   165→      )
   166→    }
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  try {
   170→    const tweet = await twitter.v2.tweet({
   171→      text: body,
   172→      ...(mediaId ? { media: { media_ids: [mediaId] } } : {}),
   173→    })
   174→    return tweet.data.id
   175→  } catch (err: unknown) {
   176→    throw new Error(`[x tweet] ${formatXApiError(err)}`)
   177→  }
   178→}
   179→

 `./worker/src/r2.ts`

```text
     1→import { PutObjectCommand, S3Client } from '@aws-sdk/client-s3'
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Same semantics as lba-social `apps/worker/src/storage/r2.ts`:
     5→ * - R2_BUCKET is either `bucket-name` or `bucket-name/optional/object/key/prefix`
     6→ *   (first path segment = real R2 bucket; rest is prepended to every object key).
     7→ * - R2_ENDPOINT optional full S3 API URL; if unset, derived from R2_ACCOUNT_ID.
     8→ * - If Access Key looks 64 chars and Secret 32 chars, credentials are swapped (paste fix).
     9→ */
    10→
    11→function splitBucket(rawBucket: string): { bucketName: string; keyPrefix: string } {
    12→  const parts = rawBucket.trim().split('/').filter(Boolean)
    13→  const bucketName = parts[0] ?? ''
    14→  const keyPrefix = parts.slice(1).join('/')
    15→  return { bucketName, keyPrefix }
    16→}
    17→
    18→function buildKey(prefix: string, key: string): string {
    19→  const k = key.replace(/^\//, '')
    20→  if (!prefix) return k
    21→  return `${prefix.replace(/\/$/, '')}/${k}`
    22→}
    23→
    24→function getR2Endpoint(): string | null {
    25→  const explicit = process.env.R2_ENDPOINT?.trim()
    26→  if (explicit) return explicit.replace(/\/$/, '')
    27→  const accountId = process.env.R2_ACCOUNT_ID?.trim()
    28→  if (accountId) return `https://${accountId}.r2.cloudflarestorage.com`
    29→  return null
    30→}
    31→
    32→function resolveCredentials(): { accessKeyId: string; secretAccessKey: string } | null {
    33→  let accessKeyId = process.env.R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID?.trim()
    34→  let secretAccessKey = process.env.R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY?.trim()
    35→  if (!accessKeyId || !secretAccessKey) return null
    36→  if (accessKeyId.length === 64 && secretAccessKey.length === 32) {
    37→    ;[accessKeyId, secretAccessKey] = [secretAccessKey, accessKeyId]
    38→    console.warn(
    39→      '[R2] R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID / R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY look reversed (32 vs 64 char lengths); using swapped values (same as lba-social worker).'
    40→    )
    41→  }
    42→  return { accessKeyId, secretAccessKey }
    43→}
    44→
    45→export function isR2Configured(): boolean {
    46→  return (
    47→    !!getR2Endpoint() &&
    48→    !!resolveCredentials() &&
    49→    !!process.env.R2_BUCKET?.trim() &&
    50→    !!splitBucket(process.env.R2_BUCKET!.trim()).bucketName &&
    51→    !!process.env.R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL?.trim()
    52→  )
    53→}
    54→
    55→function isR2AccessDenied(err: unknown): boolean {
    56→  if (!err || typeof err !== 'object') return false
    57→  const e = err as { name?: string; Code?: string }
    58→  return e.name === 'AccessDenied' || e.Code === 'AccessDenied'
    59→}
    60→
    61→function isR2NoSuchBucket(err: unknown): boolean {
    62→  if (!err || typeof err !== 'object') return false
    63→  const e = err as { name?: string; Code?: string }
    64→  return e.name === 'NoSuchBucket' || e.Code === 'NoSuchBucket'
    65→}
    66→
    67→const R2_ACCESS_DENIED_HINT =
    68→  'R2 returned Access Denied (403). In Cloudflare: R2 → Manage R2 API Tokens — create a token with **Object Read & Write** (or Admin Read & Write) on **this bucket**, or include this bucket in the token scope. Confirm R2_ENDPOINT (or R2_ACCOUNT_ID) matches the account that owns the bucket, R2_BUCKET’s **first path segment** is the exact bucket name, and the access key belongs to that token.'
    69→
    70→const R2_NO_SUCH_BUCKET_HINT =
    71→  'R2 says the bucket does not exist (NoSuchBucket). R2_BUCKET must start with the **exact** bucket name (first segment before any `/`). Extra segments are an object key prefix only. Example: `my-bucket` or `my-bucket/graphics/lba`. Create the bucket in Cloudflare R2 if needed; names are case-sensitive.'
    72→
    73→/** Upload PNG bytes; returns public URL (R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL + full object key). */
    74→export async function uploadPublicPng(key: string, body: Buffer): Promise<string> {
    75→  const endpoint = getR2Endpoint()
    76→  const creds = resolveCredentials()
    77→  const rawBucket = process.env.R2_BUCKET?.trim()
    78→  const base = process.env.R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL?.replace(/\/$/, '')
    79→  if (!endpoint || !creds || !rawBucket || !base) {
    80→    throw new Error('R2 is not fully configured (bucket / public URL / credentials)')
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  const { bucketName, keyPrefix } = splitBucket(rawBucket)
    84→  if (!bucketName) {
    85→    throw new Error('R2_BUCKET is empty or invalid')
    86→  }
    87→
    88→  const client = new S3Client({
    89→    region: 'auto',
    90→    endpoint,
    91→    credentials: creds,
    92→  })
    93→
    94→  const objectKey = buildKey(keyPrefix, key)
    95→
    96→  try {
    97→    await client.send(
    98→      new PutObjectCommand({
    99→        Bucket: bucketName,
   100→        Key: objectKey,
   101→        Body: body,
   102→        ContentType: 'image/png',
   103→      })
   104→    )
   105→  } catch (err) {
   106→    if (isR2AccessDenied(err)) {
   107→      console.error('[R2] PutObject AccessDenied', {
   108→        bucketName,
   109→        keyPrefix: keyPrefix || '(none)',
   110→        objectKeyPrefix: objectKey.slice(0, 64),
   111→      })
   112→      throw new Error(R2_ACCESS_DENIED_HINT)
   113→    }
   114→    if (isR2NoSuchBucket(err)) {
   115→      console.error('[R2] PutObject NoSuchBucket', { bucketName, keyPrefix })
   116→      throw new Error(R2_NO_SUCH_BUCKET_HINT)
   117→    }
   118→    throw err
   119→  }
   120→
   121→  return `${base}/${objectKey}`
   122→}
   123→
```
</details></tool-use>

    33→
    34→export async function resolvePostBody(post: ScheduledPost): Promise<string> {
    35→  if (post.caption?.trim()) return buildFinalCaption(post, post.caption)
    36→
    37→  if (post.post_type === 'verified_game' && post.match_id) {
    38→    return buildGameResultCaption(post)
    39→  }
    40→
    41→  if (post.post_type === 'final_score') {
    42→    return buildFinalCaption(post, await buildFinalScoreCaption(post))
    43→  }
    44→
    45→  if (post.post_type === 'player_of_game') {
    46→    return buildFinalCaption(post, buildPlayerOfGameCaption(post.payload_json))
    47→  }
    48→
    49→  if (post.post_type === 'weekly_power_rankings') {
    50→    return buildFinalCaption(post, buildPowerRankingsCaption(post.payload_json))
    51→  }
    52→
    53→  if (post.post_type.startsWith('announcement_')) {
    54→    const kind = postTypeToKind(post.post_type)
    55→    if (!kind) {
    56→      throw new Error(`Unknown announcement post type: ${post.post_type}`)
    57→    }
    58→    const p = post.payload_json
    59→    const payload: AnnouncementPayload = {
    60→      season:
    61→        typeof p.season === 'string' && p.season.trim()
    62→          ? p.season.trim()
    63→          : 'Season',
    64→      season_id: typeof p.season_id === 'string' ? p.season_id : undefined,
    65→      draft_date: typeof p.draft_date === 'string' ? p.draft_date : undefined,
    66→      combine_dates: typeof p.combine_dates === 'string' ? p.combine_dates : undefined,
    67→      prize_pool: typeof p.prize_pool === 'string' ? p.prize_pool : undefined,
    68→      cta: typeof p.cta === 'string' && p.cta.trim() ? p.cta.trim() : '',
    69→      cta_label: typeof p.cta_label === 'string' ? p.cta_label : undefined,
    70→      league_logo: typeof p.league_logo === 'string' ? p.league_logo : null,
    71→      vibe: normalizeVibe(typeof p.vibe === 'string' ? p.vibe : undefined),
    72→      headline_override:
    73→        typeof p.headline_override === 'string' ? p.headline_override : undefined,
    74→      result_lines: Array.isArray(p.result_lines)
    75→        ? p.result_lines.filter((x): x is string => typeof x === 'string')
    76→        : undefined,
    77→      champion_team:
    78→        typeof p.champion_team === 'string' && p.champion_team.trim()
    79→          ? p.champion_team.trim()
    80→          : undefined,
    81→      series_score:
    82→        typeof p.series_score === 'string' && p.series_score.trim()
    83→          ? p.series_score.trim()
    84→          : undefined,
    85→      award_name:
    86→        typeof p.award_name === 'string' && p.award_name.trim()
    87→          ? p.award_name.trim()
    88→          : undefined,
    89→      recipient_name:

    91→          ? p.recipient_name.trim()
    92→          : undefined,
    93→      recipient_stats:

    99→          ? p.game_count.trim()

   107→          ? p.bracket_size.trim()
   108→          : undefined,
   109→    }
   110→    return buildFinalCaption(post, buildAnnouncementCaption(kind, payload))
   111→  }
   112→
   113→  if (post.payload_json.body && typeof post.payload_json.body === 'string') {
   114→    return buildFinalCaption(post, post.payload_json.body)
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  throw new Error('No post body could be resolved')
   118→}

   144→      )

   150→      const away = m.team_b?.name ?? '?'

    72→/** ISO BMFF / HEIF (incl. AVIF); catches `.webp` URLs that serve AVIF bytes. */
    73→function bufferLooksLikeHeifOrAvif(buf: Buffer): boolean {
    74→  if (buf.length < 12) return false

    76→  const brand = buf.subarray(8, 12).toString('ascii')

    84→  return buf.subarray(8, 12).toString('ascii')

     1→import { composeAiPostGraphic } from '../card-generator.js'
     2→import { uploadPublicPng } from '../r2.js'
     3→import { sha256Hex } from './hash.js'
     4→import { buildBgPrompt, normalizeStylePack } from './bgPrompts.js'
     5→import { generateImage } from './imageClient.js'
     6→
     7→export const AI_IMAGE_POST_TYPES = [
     8→  'final_score',
     9→  'player_of_game',
    10→  'weekly_power_rankings',
    11→  'announcement_registration',
    12→  'announcement_draft',
    13→  'announcement_results',
    14→  'announcement_playoffs',
    15→  'announcement_champion',
    16→  'announcement_awards',
    17→  'announcement_schedule',
    18→] as const
    19→
    20→export type AiImagePostType = (typeof AI_IMAGE_POST_TYPES)[number]
    21→
    22→export function isAiImagePostType(postType: string): postType is AiImagePostType {
    23→  return (AI_IMAGE_POST_TYPES as readonly string[]).includes(postType)
    24→}
    25→
    26→export function getBackgroundCacheKey(
    27→  postType: string,
    28→  stylePack: string,
    29→  styleVersion: number,
    30→  payload: Record<string, unknown>
    31→): string {
    32→  const parts: string[] = [postType, stylePack, String(styleVersion)]
    33→  if (postType === 'weekly_power_rankings' && payload.week_label) {
    34→    parts.push(String(payload.week_label))
    35→  }
    36→  if (
    37→    (postType === 'final_score' || postType === 'player_of_game') &&
    38→    payload.match_id
    39→  ) {
    40→    parts.push(String(payload.match_id))
    41→  }
    42→  if (
    43→    postType.startsWith('announcement_') &&
    44→    (payload.season_id || payload.season)
    45→  ) {
    46→    parts.push(String(payload.season_id ?? payload.season))
    47→    parts.push(String(payload.vibe ?? ''))
    48→    parts.push(String(payload.draft_date ?? ''))
    49→    parts.push(String(payload.combine_dates ?? ''))
    50→    parts.push(String(payload.prize_pool ?? ''))
    51→    parts.push(String(payload.headline_override ?? ''))
    52→    parts.push(String(payload.champion_team ?? ''))
    53→    parts.push(String(payload.award_name ?? ''))
    54→    parts.push(String(payload.recipient_name ?? ''))
    55→  }
    56→  return sha256Hex(parts.join(':'))
    57→}
    58→
    59→export interface GenerateBackgroundForPostParams {
    60→  postType: string
    61→  stylePack: string
    62→  styleVersion: number
    63→  payload: Record<string, unknown>
    64→}
    65→
    66→export interface GenerateBackgroundForPostResult {
    67→  imageUrl: string
    68→  prompt: string
    69→}
    70→
    71→/** Build prompt, generate image, upload to R2 under social-poster/bg/… */
    72→export async function generateBackgroundForPost(
    73→  params: GenerateBackgroundForPostParams
    74→): Promise<GenerateBackgroundForPostResult> {
    75→  const stylePack = normalizeStylePack(params.stylePack)
    76→  const prompt = buildBgPrompt({
    77→    postType: params.postType,
    78→    stylePack,
    79→    payload: params.payload,
    80→  })
    81→  const buffer = await generateImage(prompt)
    82→  let outBuf = buffer
    83→  try {
    84→    outBuf = await composeAiPostGraphic(
    85→      params.postType,
    86→      params.payload,
    87→      buffer
    88→    )
    89→  } catch (e) {
    90→    console.warn('[ai] compose graphic failed, using raw background:', e)
    91→  }
    92→  const cacheKey = getBackgroundCacheKey(
    93→    params.postType,
    94→    stylePack,
    95→    params.styleVersion,
    96→    params.payload
    97→  )
    98→  const key = `social-poster/bg/${stylePack}/${cacheKey}.png`
    99→  const imageUrl = await uploadPublicPng(key, outBuf)
   100→  return { imageUrl, prompt }
   101→}
   102→

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worker/src/ai/imageClient.ts`

```text
     1→import { generateImageImagen } from './imagenClient.js'
     2→
     3→const IMAGE_PROVIDER_OPENAI = 'openai'
     4→const IMAGE_PROVIDER_GEMINI = 'gemini'
     5→
     6→const DEFAULT_BASE_URL = 'https://api.openai.com/v1'
     7→const DEFAULT_MODEL = 'gpt-image-1'
     8→const DEFAULT_SIZE = '1024x1536'
     9→const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS = 60_000
    10→const MAX_RETRIES = 2
    11→
    12→interface ImagesGenerationsResponse {
    13→  data?: Array<{ b64_json?: string; url?: string }>
    14→  error?: { message?: string }
    15→}
    16→
    17→function getImageProvider(): 'openai' | 'gemini' {
    18→  const v = process.env.AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER?.toLowerCase().trim()
    19→  if (v === IMAGE_PROVIDER_GEMINI) return 'gemini'
    20→  return IMAGE_PROVIDER_OPENAI
    21→}
    22→
    23→/** Dispatch to OpenAI Images or Imagen (Gemini) per AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER. Returns PNG bytes. */
    24→export async function generateImage(prompt: string): Promise<Buffer> {
    25→  const provider = getImageProvider()
    26→  if (provider === IMAGE_PROVIDER_GEMINI) {
    27→    return generateImageImagen(prompt)
    28→  }
    29→  return generateImageOpenAI(prompt)
    30→}
    31→
    32→async function generateImageOpenAI(prompt: string): Promise<Buffer> {
    33→  const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
    34→  if (!apiKey) {
    35→    throw new Error('OPENAI_API_KEY must be set for image generation')
    36→  }
    37→
    38→  const baseUrl = (process.env.OPENAI_BASE_URL ?? DEFAULT_BASE_URL).replace(/\/$/, '')
    39→  const model = process.env.OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL ?? DEFAULT_MODEL
    40→  const size = process.env.OPENAI_IMAGE_SIZE ?? DEFAULT_SIZE
    41→  const quality = process.env.OPENAI_IMAGE_QUALITY
    42→
    43→  const url = `${baseUrl}/images/generations`
    44→  const body: Record<string, unknown> = {
    45→    model,
    46→    prompt,
    47→    size,
    48→    /** Without this, OpenAI often returns `url` only and we would fail on b64_json. */
    49→    response_format: 'b64_json',
    50→  }
    51→  if (quality) body.quality = quality
    52→
    53→  let lastErr: unknown
    54→  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= MAX_RETRIES; attempt++) {
    55→    try {
    56→      const controller = new AbortController()
    57→      const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS)
    58→      const response = await fetch(url, {
    59→        method: 'POST',
    60→        headers: {
    61→          Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
    62→          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    63→        },
    64→        body: JSON.stringify(body),
    65→        signal: controller.signal,
    66→      })
    67→      clearTimeout(timeout)
    68→
    69→      const data = (await response.json()) as ImagesGenerationsResponse
    70→      if (data.error?.message) {
    71→        throw new Error(`OpenAI Images API: ${data.error.message}`)
    72→      }
    73→      if (!response.ok) {
    74→        throw new Error(`OpenAI Images API ${response.status}: ${JSON.stringify(data)}`)
    75→      }
    76→      const first = data.data?.[0]
    77→      const b64 = first?.b64_json
    78→      if (b64) {
    79→        return Buffer.from(b64, 'base64')
    80→      }
    81→      const imageUrl = first?.url
    82→      if (imageUrl) {
    83→        const imgRes = await fetch(imageUrl)
    84→        if (!imgRes.ok) {
    85→          throw new Error(
    86→            `OpenAI returned image URL but download failed: HTTP ${imgRes.status}`
    87→          )
    88→        }
    89→        return Buffer.from(await imgRes.arrayBuffer())
    90→      }
    91→      throw new Error(
    92→        'OpenAI Images API: response had no b64_json or url in data[0]'
    93→      )
    94→    } catch (err) {
    95→      lastErr = err
    96→      if (attempt === MAX_RETRIES) break
    97→      await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1000 * attempt))
    98→    }
    99→  }
   100→  throw lastErr
   101→}
   102→
```
</details></tool-use>

    16→  buildAnnouncementCaption,
    17→  buildMidjourneyPromptExport,
    18→  kindToPostType,
    19→  normalizeVibe,
    20→} from './announcements/templates.js'
    21→import { supabase } from './db.js'
    22→import { fetchLeagueLogoUrlByLeagueId } from './leagueLogo.js'
    23→import { isR2Configured } from './r2.js'
    24→import { planPosts } from './planning/planPosts.js'
    25→import {
    26→  existsAnnouncementScheduled,
    27→  fetchStudioSuggestions,
    28→} from './planning/queries.js'
    29→import {
    30→  getXAppConsumerKeys,

    33→import {
    34→  signXOAuthPayload,
    35→  verifyXOAuthCookie,
    36→  xOAuthCookieHeader,
    37→  type XOAuthPendingPayload,
    38→} from './xOAuth.js'
    39→
    40→const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
    41→
    42→const ADMIN_SECRET = process.env.ADMIN_SECRET ?? 'changeme'
    43→
    44→const UUID_RE =
    45→  /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[1-5][0-9a-f]{3}-[89ab][0-9a-f]{3}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i
    46→
    47→function isUuid(s: string): boolean {
    48→  return UUID_RE.test(s.trim())
    49→}
    50→
    51→function parseAnnouncementKindParam(seg: string | undefined): AnnouncementKind | null {
    52→  const s = (seg ?? '').trim().toLowerCase()
    53→  if (s === 'registration') return 'registration'
    54→  if (s === 'draft') return 'draft'
    55→  if (s === 'results') return 'results'
    56→  if (s === 'playoffs') return 'playoffs'
    57→  if (s === 'champion') return 'champion'
    58→  if (s === 'awards') return 'awards'
    59→  if (s === 'schedule') return 'schedule'
    60→  return null
    61→}
    62→
    63→function getR2ConfigError(): string | null {
    64→  const missing: string[] = []
    65→  if (!process.env.R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID?.trim()) missing.push('R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID')
    66→  if (!process.env.R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY?.trim()) missing.push('R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY')
    67→  if (!process.env.R2_BUCKET?.trim()) missing.push('R2_BUCKET')
    68→  if (!process.env.R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL?.trim()) missing.push('R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL')
    69→  const hasEndpoint = !!process.env.R2_ENDPOINT?.trim()
    70→  const hasAccount = !!process.env.R2_ACCOUNT_ID?.trim()
    71→  if (!hasEndpoint && !hasAccount) {
    72→    missing.push('R2_ENDPOINT or R2_ACCOUNT_ID')
    73→  }
    74→  if (missing.length === 0) return null
    75→  return `R2 is not fully configured. Missing or empty env: ${missing.join(', ')}. Same shape as lba-social: optional R2_ENDPOINT, or R2_ACCOUNT_ID to build the S3 endpoint; R2_BUCKET = bucket name or bucket/prefix.`
    76→}
    77→
    78→function getAiImageApiConfigError(): string | null {
    79→  const p = process.env.AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER?.toLowerCase().trim()
    80→  const useGemini = p === 'gemini'
    81→  if (useGemini) {
    82→    if (!process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY?.trim()) {
    83→      return 'AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER=gemini but GEMINI_API_KEY is missing or empty.'
    84→    }
    85→    return null
    86→  }
    87→  if (!process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY?.trim()) {
    88→    return 'OPENAI_API_KEY is missing or empty (default image provider is OpenAI). For Imagen, set AI_IMAGE_PROVIDER=gemini and GEMINI_API_KEY.'
    89→  }
    90→  return null
    91→}
    92→
    93→function oauthCallbackUrl(c: { req: { url: string } }): string {
    94→  const explicit = process.env.X_OAUTH_CALLBACK_URL?.trim()
    95→  if (explicit) return explicit.replace(/\/$/, '')
    96→  return new URL(c.req.url).origin + '/api/x/oauth/callback'
    97→}
    98→
    99→function adminRedirectUrl(c: { req: { url: string } }, query: string): string {
   100→  const origin = new URL(c.req.url).origin
   101→  return `${origin}/admin${query ? `?${query}` : ''}`
   102→}
   103→
   104→function normalizeTeamEmbed(v: unknown): { name: string } | null {
   105→  if (v == null) return null
   106→  if (Array.isArray(v)) {
   107→    const x = v[0]
   108→    if (x && typeof x === 'object' && x !== null && 'name' in x) {
   109→      return { name: String((x as { name: unknown }).name) }
   110→    }
   111→    return null
   112→  }
   113→  if (typeof v === 'object' && 'name' in v) {
   114→    return { name: String((v as { name: unknown }).name) }
   115→  }
   116→  return null
   117→}
   118→
   119→const authMiddleware = createMiddleware(async (c, next) => {
   120→  const auth = c.req.header('Authorization') ?? ''
   121→  const token = auth.replace(/^Bearer\s+/i, '')
   122→  if (token !== ADMIN_SECRET) {
   123→    return c.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, 401)
   124→  }
   125→  await next()
   126→})
   127→
   128→export function createServer() {
   129→  const app = new Hono()
   130→
   131→  app.get('/health', c => c.text('ok'))
   132→
   133→  app.get('/favicon.ico', () => {
   134→    const svg =
   135→      '<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><rect width="32" height="32" rx="6" fill="#7c6af7"/></svg>'
   136→    return new Response(svg, {
   137→      headers: {
   138→        'Content-Type': 'image/svg+xml',
   139→        'Cache-Control': 'public, max-age=86400',
   140→      },
   141→    })
   142→  })
   143→
   144→  app.get('/admin', c => {
   145→    const path = join(__dirname, '../public/admin.html')
   146→    const html = readFileSync(path, 'utf8')
   147→    return c.html(html)
   148→  })
   149→
   150→  app.get('/api/posts', authMiddleware, async c => {
   151→    const mode = c.req.query('mode') ?? 'queue'
   152→    const statusFilter =
   153→      mode === 'queue'
   154→        ? ['pending', 'processing', 'scheduled', 'draft']
   155→        : ['published', 'failed']
   156→
   157→    // No FK scheduled_posts.match_id → matches.id in some DBs → PostgREST cannot embed `matches`.
   158→    // Fetch posts, then matches in a second query and merge (same shape the admin UI expects).
   159→    const { data: posts, error } = await supabase
   160→      .from('scheduled_posts')
   161→      .select(
   162→        `
   163→        id, post_type, status, caption, hashtags, scheduled_for,
   164→        bg_image_url, x_post_id, error, retries, created_at, match_id,
   165→        payload_json
   166→      `
   167→      )
   168→      .in('status', statusFilter)
   169→      .contains('publish_surface', ['x'])
   170→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
   171→      .limit(50)
   172→
   173→    if (error) return c.json({ error: error.message }, 500)
   174→
   175→    const list = posts ?? []
   176→    const matchIds = [
   177→      ...new Set(
   178→        list
   179→          .map(p => p.match_id)
   180→          .filter((id): id is string => typeof id === 'string' && id.length > 0)
   181→      ),
   182→    ]
   183→
   184→    const matchById = new Map<
   185→      string,
   186→      {
   187→        score_a: number | null
   188→        score_b: number | null
   189→        team_a: { name: string } | null
   190→        team_b: { name: string } | null
   191→      }
   192→    >()
   193→
   194→    if (matchIds.length > 0) {
   195→      const { data: matches, error: mErr } = await supabase
   196→        .from('matches')
   197→        .select(
   198→          `
   199→          id, score_a, score_b,
   200→          team_a:teams!team_a_id(name),
   201→          team_b:teams!team_b_id(name)
   202→        `
   203→        )
   204→        .in('id', matchIds)
   205→
   206→      if (mErr) return c.json({ error: mErr.message }, 500)
   207→
   208→      for (const m of matches ?? []) {
   209→        const row = m as {
   210→          id: string
   211→          score_a: number | null
   212→          score_b: number | null
   213→          team_a: unknown
   214→          team_b: unknown
   215→        }
   216→        matchById.set(row.id, {
   217→          score_a: row.score_a,
   218→          score_b: row.score_b,
   219→          team_a: normalizeTeamEmbed(row.team_a),
   220→          team_b: normalizeTeamEmbed(row.team_b),
   221→        })
   222→      }
   223→    }
   224→
   225→    const enriched = list.map(p => ({
   226→      ...p,
   227→      matches:
   228→        p.match_id && matchById.has(p.match_id)
   229→          ? matchById.get(p.match_id)!
   230→          : null,
   231→    }))
   232→
   233→    return c.json(enriched)
   234→  })
   235→
   236→  app.patch('/api/posts/:id', authMiddleware, async c => {
   237→    const id = c.req.param('id')
   238→    let body: Record<string, unknown>
   239→    try {
   240→      body = await c.req.json()
   241→    } catch {
   242→      return c.json({ error: 'Invalid JSON' }, 400)
   243→    }
   244→    const allowed = ['caption', 'status', 'scheduled_for', 'hashtags'] as const
   245→    const patch: Record<string, unknown> = { updated_at: new Date().toISOString() }
   246→    for (const key of allowed) {
   247→      if (!(key in body)) continue
   248→      if (key === 'scheduled_for') {
   249→        const v = body.scheduled_for
   250→        if (v == null || v === '') {
   251→          patch.scheduled_for = new Date().toISOString()
   252→        } else if (typeof v === 'string') {
   253→          patch.scheduled_for = new Date(v).toISOString()
   254→        } else {
   255→          patch.scheduled_for = v
   256→        }
   257→        continue
   258→      }
   259→      patch[key] = body[key]
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    if ('bg_image_url' in body) {
   263→      const v = body.bg_image_url
   264→      if (v === null || v === '') patch.bg_image_url = null
   265→      else if (typeof v === 'string') patch.bg_image_url = v.trim() || null
   266→    }
   267→
   268→    const pp = body.payload_patch
   269→    if (
   270→      pp !== null &&
   271→      pp !== undefined &&
   272→      typeof pp === 'object' &&
   273→      !Array.isArray(pp)
   274→    ) {
   275→      const { data: row, error: selErr } = await supabase
   276→        .from('scheduled_posts')
   277→        .select('payload_json')
   278→        .eq('id', id)
   279→        .single()
   280→      if (selErr) return c.json({ error: selErr.message }, 500)
   281→      const raw = row?.payload_json
   282→      const cur: Record<string, unknown> =
   283→        raw !== null &&
   284→        raw !== undefined &&
   285→        typeof raw === 'object' &&
   286→        !Array.isArray(raw)
   287→          ? { ...(raw as Record<string, unknown>) }
   288→          : {}
   289→      const incoming = pp as Record<string, unknown>
   290→      for (const k of ['generate_image', 'style_pack', 'style_version'] as const) {
   291→        if (k in incoming) cur[k] = incoming[k]
   292→      }
   293→      patch.payload_json = cur
   294→    }
   295→
   296→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   297→      .from('scheduled_posts')
   298→      .update(patch)
   299→      .eq('id', id)
   300→      .select()
```

/**
 * League announcement templates: copy, AI scene fragments, optional Midjourney export strings.
 * Image models stay text-free; typography is composited via Satori in card-generator.
 */

export type AnnouncementKind =
  | 'registration'
  | 'draft'
  | 'results'
  | 'playoffs'
  | 'champion'
  | 'awards'
  | 'schedule'

export type AnnouncementVibe =
  | 'esports_2k'
  | 'luxury'
  | 'hype'
  | 'broadcast'
  | 'championship'
  | 'cartoon_modern'

export const ANNOUNCEMENT_POST_TYPES = [
  'announcement_registration',
  'announcement_draft',
  'announcement_results',
  'announcement_playoffs',
  'announcement_champion',
  'announcement_awards',
  'announcement_schedule',
] as const

export type AnnouncementPostType = (typeof ANNOUNCEMENT_POST_TYPES)[number]

export interface AnnouncementPayload {
  /** Display season label, e.g. "Season 2" or "2" */
  season: string
  /** League season row id — used for dedupe and automation */
  season_id?: string
  draft_date?: string
  combine_dates?: string
  /** Human-readable e.g. "$1,500" */
  prize_pool?: string
  /** URL or path for signup (shown on graphic and caption) */
  cta: string
  cta_label?: string
  league_logo?: string | null
  vibe?: AnnouncementVibe
  headline_override?: string
  /** Extra lines for results (e.g. champion, standings note) */
```

     1→import {
     2→  existsScheduledByPayload,
     3→  fetchCompletedMatches,
     4→  fetchMatchesWithMvp,
     5→  fetchTop10PowerRankings,
     6→  getLeagueId,
     7→  insertPlannedXPost,
     8→} from './queries.js'
     9→import type {
    10→  FinalScorePayload,
    11→  PlayerOfGamePayload,
    12→  PowerRankingsPayload,
    13→} from './payloads.js'
    14→
    15→const POST_DELAY_MINUTES = 30
    16→const POWER_RANKINGS_DAY = parseInt(process.env.POWER_RANKINGS_DAY ?? '0', 10)
    17→const POWER_RANKINGS_HOUR = parseInt(process.env.POWER_RANKINGS_HOUR ?? '18', 10)
    18→
    19→function getNextPowerRankingsTime(): Date {
    20→  const now = new Date()
    21→  const next = new Date(now)
    22→  next.setHours(POWER_RANKINGS_HOUR, 0, 0, 0)
    23→  next.setDate(next.getDate() + ((7 + POWER_RANKINGS_DAY - next.getDay()) % 7))
    24→  if (next <= now) next.setDate(next.getDate() + 7)
    25→  return next
    26→}
    27→
    28→function formatDate(iso: string): string {
    29→  const d = new Date(iso)
    30→  return d.toISOString().split('T')[0]
    31→}
    32→
    33→const DEFAULT_PLAN_STYLE =
    34→  process.env.PLAN_DEFAULT_STYLE_PACK?.trim() || 'regular'
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Plan X posts from league data (final scores, POG, power rankings).
    38→ * Requires LEAGUE_ID. Power rankings need lba_teams + league_conference_standings.
    39→ */
    40→export async function planPosts(): Promise<{ inserted: number; errors: string[] }> {
    41→  const leagueId = getLeagueId()
    42→  const errors: string[] = []
    43→  let inserted = 0
    44→
    45→  console.log('[plan] starting planPosts')
    46→
    47→  try {
    48→    const matches = await fetchCompletedMatches(leagueId, 10)
    49→    console.log('[plan] completed matches', matches.length)
    50→
    51→    for (const m of matches) {
    52→      const scheduledFor = new Date(m.played_at ?? m.verified_at ?? Date.now())
    53→      scheduledFor.setMinutes(scheduledFor.getMinutes() + POST_DELAY_MINUTES)
    54→
    55→      const payload: FinalScorePayload = {
    56→        match_id: m.id,
    57→        home_team: m.home_team,
    58→        away_team: m.away_team,
    59→        home_score: m.score_a ?? 0,
    60→        away_score: m.score_b ?? 0,
    61→        date: formatDate(
    62→          m.played_at ?? m.verified_at ?? new Date().toISOString()
    63→        ),
    64→        home_team_logo: m.home_team_logo,
    65→        away_team_logo: m.away_team_logo,
    66→        league_logo: m.league_logo,
    67→        boxscore_url: m.boxscore_url ?? null,
    68→      }
    69→
    70→      if (await existsScheduledByPayload('final_score', payload)) continue
    71→
    72→      await insertPlannedXPost(
    73→        'final_score',
    74→        scheduledFor.toISOString(),
    75→        payload,
    76→        null,
    77→        { style_pack: DEFAULT_PLAN_STYLE }
    78→      )
    79→      inserted++
    80→    }
    81→  } catch (e) {
    82→    const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)
    83→    errors.push(`final_score: ${msg}`)
    84→    console.error('[plan] final_score block failed:', e)
    85→  }
    86→
    87→  try {
    88→    const mvps = await fetchMatchesWithMvp(leagueId, 10)
    89→    console.log('[plan] matches with MVP', mvps.length)
    90→
    91→    for (const m of mvps) {
    92→      const scheduledFor = new Date(m.played_at)
    93→      scheduledFor.setMinutes(scheduledFor.getMinutes() + POST_DELAY_MINUTES + 5)
    94→
    95→      const payload: PlayerOfGamePayload = {
    96→        match_id: m.match_id,
    97→        player_name: m.player_name,
    98→        stat_line: m.stat_line,
    99→        team_name: m.team_name,
   100→        date: formatDate(m.played_at),
   101→        team_logo: m.team_logo,
   102→        league_logo: m.league_logo,
   103→      }
   104→
   105→      if (await existsScheduledByPayload('player_of_game', payload)) continue
   106→
   107→      await insertPlannedXPost(
   108→        'player_of_game',
   109→        scheduledFor.toISOString(),
   110→        payload,
   111→        null,
   112→        { style_pack: DEFAULT_PLAN_STYLE }
   113→      )
   114→      inserted++
   115→    }
   116→  } catch (e) {
   117→    const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)
   118→    errors.push(`player_of_game: ${msg}`)
   119→    console.error('[plan] player_of_game block failed:', e)
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  try {
   123→    const nextPr = getNextPowerRankingsTime()
   124→    const weekLabel = `Week ${Math.ceil(nextPr.getDate() / 7)}`
   125→
   126→    const pr = await fetchTop10PowerRankings(leagueId)
   127→    if (pr.teams.length >= 1) {
   128→      const payload: PowerRankingsPayload = {
   129→        week_label: weekLabel,
   130→        league_logo: pr.league_logo,
   131→        teams: pr.teams.map(t => ({
   132→          rank: t.rank,
   133→          team_name: t.name,
   134→          record: t.record,
   135→          team_logo: t.team_logo,
   136→        })),
   137→      }
   138→
   139→      if (!(await existsScheduledByPayload('weekly_power_rankings', payload))) {
   140→        await insertPlannedXPost(
   141→          'weekly_power_rankings',
   142→          nextPr.toISOString(),
   143→          payload,
   144→          null,
   145→          { style_pack: DEFAULT_PLAN_STYLE }
   146→        )
   147→        inserted++
   148→      }
   149→    }
   150→  } catch (e) {
   151→    const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)
   152→    errors.push(`weekly_power_rankings: ${msg}`)
   153→    console.error('[plan] power rankings block failed:', e)
   154→  }
   155→
   156→  console.log('[plan] complete', { inserted, errors: errors.length })
   157→  return { inserted, errors }
   158→}
   159→
```
</details></tool-use>

    74→  const brand = brandDirective()
